query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
(41, 'According to the biblical and historical evidence discussed in this lesson, which statement best describes the formation of the New Testament canon?', 'multiple_choice', '["The canon was arbitrarily determined by church councils in the 4th century", "The canon emerged through the Spirit-led recognition of apostolic authority and divine inspiration by the early church", "The canon was politically imposed by Constantine", "The canon remains disputed among all Christian traditions today"]', 'The canon emerged through the Spirit-led recognition of apostolic authority and divine inspiration by the early church', 1),
(41, 'Which historical and cultural factor most significantly shaped the context in which the New Testament was written?', 'multiple_choice', '["The Roman Empire''s political structure, Greek language and culture, and Jewish religious heritage", "Exclusively Jewish religious practices with no Gentile influence", "Greek philosophy alone without Jewish or Roman elements", "Medieval European theological frameworks"]', 'The Roman Empire''s political structure, Greek language and culture, and Jewish religious heritage', 2),
(41, 'How does understanding the historical-cultural context of the New Testament enhance biblical interpretation and application?', 'multiple_choice', '["Historical context is irrelevant to spiritual truth", "Context helps us distinguish timeless principles from cultural expressions while maintaining biblical authority", "Context undermines the authority of Scripture", "Context only matters for academic study, not practical ministry"]', 'Context helps us distinguish timeless principles from cultural expressions while maintaining biblical authority', 3),
(41, 'True or False: The New Testament canon was universally recognized immediately after the apostolic age with no development or discussion in the early church.', 'true_false', '["True", "False"]', 'False', 4),
(41, 'In 2-3 sentences, explain how the historical development of the New Testament canon demonstrates both divine sovereignty and human agency in the preservation of Scripture.', 'short_answer', NULL, 'The formation of the New Testament canon involved the Holy Spirit guiding the early church to recognize which writings bore apostolic authority and divine inspiration (2 Peter 1:20-21). While human church leaders engaged in careful discernment and theological reflection, God sovereignly preserved His Word through this process. This demonstrates that biblical inspiration extends not only to the original writing but also to the providential preservation and recognition of canonical Scripture.', 5)
